steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence says using "philo-Publius" accomplished "far more than simply concealing his authorship." The blank sentence must introduce an explanation of what that "far more" actually was — the relationship is elaboration or confirmation, not contrast or addition.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Conversely,: Signals a contradiction or reversal. The second sentence elaborates on the first — it doesn't contradict it. ✗
  • Choice B — Indeed,: Signals reinforcement and confirmation — the second sentence fulfills the promise of "far more" by explaining the deliberate rhetorical strategy. Fits perfectly. ✓
  • Choice C — In addition,: Signals a new, separate point being added. The second sentence isn't adding a new idea — it's explaining the idea already introduced. ✗
  • Choice D — However,: Signals a contrast or concession. No contrast exists between the two sentences; they move in the same direction. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
